_comment: File-major casualty reflow (#878): two identical Spearmen-vs-Infantry standoffs, side by side, staged fresh for this demo (not a copy of spear-standoff.json). The LEFT pair's Spearmen keeps the new default (file_major_reform: true): each soldier holds a persistent file assignment, so a casualty only closes the gap within its own column, leaving the survivors' rear jagged -- some files run deeper than others. The RIGHT pair's Spearmen explicitly opts out (file_major_reform: false, the historical behavior): the whole grid recomputes fresh from the live headcount every tick, so the rear reflows and re-centres uniformly regardless of which files actually took the losses. Both Infantry attackers march in and lock melee under their own enemy-AI order -- no scripted steps needed.
